summary: Juridical charge sheet from the Museo Temple Radicati — same museum as UR110 and UR112. 26 cords, 8 STRING (31%), 88% hits. Architecture 0-SEUL, depth L1, 6 clusters, 5 colors. FB(7)+PR(7) = action colors dominate. Date: 1504/09/07 CE Mode A. Key vocabulary: taka x2 (to strike), wama x1 (FALCON — same word as UR176 'The Falcon of Chuquitanta'), waki x1 (orphan/cooperation), takaki x1 (strike-POSS). The falcon returns alongside violence — this is not a trial but an accusation, a charge sheet.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  26 cords, depth L1 — flat structure, no subsidiaries.
  Architecture: 0-SEUL, 6 clusters.
  Colors: FB(7), PR(7), W(3), HB(3), RB(2) — 5 colors.
  8 STRING (31%), 88% hit rate. 16 INT, 2 EMPTY.
  Date: 1504/09/07 CE Mode A.
  Provenance: Unknown.
  Museum: Museo Temple Radicati (same museum as UR110 and UR112 — juridical khipus).

COLOR-AS-ROLE
  FB (light brown, 7 cords = 27%): Action/evidence color — verbs, material facts.
  PR (purple, 7 cords = 27%): Action/evidence color — verbs, material evidence.
  W (white, 3 cords): Neutral/data color.
  HB (honey brown, 3 cords): Secondary category.
  RB (red-brown, 2 cords): Alert/flag color.
  FB+PR dominant = ALL ACTION. No LK (black/governance) at all — this is not a governance document. It is an action document.

KEY VOCABULARY
  taka x2 = to strike (the charge, twice stated)
  waki x1 = orphan/cooperation (a party? a context?)
  ta x1 = accusative suffix or root
  chaki x1 = foot/Scorpio (proper name or epithet — 'the foot')
  wama x1 = FALCON/hawk (the same word that named the accused in UR176)
  takaki x1 = taka+ki = strike-POSS = 'of the strike'

OKR NOTES
  C12: 'Has same appearance of barber pole' = STRIPED cord — physical encoding of duality or conflict.
  C15: 'Splits in exact middle' = physically divided cord — a cord that breaks into two halves.

CROSS-CORPUS — THE FALCON RETURNS
  In UR176 ('The Falcon of Chuquitanta'), wama named the accused in a murder case. Here again, the falcon appears alongside violence: taka x2 + wama. The Museo Temple Radicati holds UR110 (complete juridical dossier, 7 colors) and UR112 (concealment case, blind replication of UR039) — all three are juridical. UR095 is the third juridical khipu from this museum.

NARRATIVE
  The falcon returns. Wama — the same word that named the accused in UR176's murder case at Chuquitanta — appears here on a khipu of twenty-six cords at the Museo Temple Radicati, the same museum that holds UR110 and UR112. The strike (taka) is named twice, and the falcon once. A striped cord at position 12 — the investigator noted it looked like a barber pole — and a cord that splits in its exact middle at position 15. The colors are all action: light brown (FB) and purple (PR) dominate, the two colors associated with verbs and material evidence. No black thread, no governance vocabulary. This is not a trial — it is a charge sheet, an accusation, and the falcon is named.

  In the context of taka and wama, the word chaki — which can mean 'foot' or name the constellation Scorpio — likely functions as a proper name or epithet. 'The Foot' — a nickname, perhaps, for a person identified by a limp or a role. And waki, orphan or cooperation, places the scene: a vulnerable party, or a communal obligation violated.

  September 1504. A year before HP051's accounting, someone at the Museo Temple Radicati's source community recorded an accusation. The falcon struck. The barber-pole cord marks the disputed evidence. The split cord divides the case in two. And the scribe, working in action colors with no governance black, wrote not a verdict but a charge — the document that begins a trial, not the one that ends it.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Juridical charge sheet analysis, falcon cross-corpus identification
